content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 30, 1990


Robert Leidigh
Olson, Connelly, Hagel, Fong & Leidigh
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Conflict of Interest Code
Our File No. I-90-200

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

We have received a request from the Enforcement Division of
the Fair Political Practices Commission to review the conflict of
interest code for the Monterey Peninsula Regional Park District.
The District is commended for incorporating Commission Regulation
18730 as the body of its code. By incorporating this regulation
the District can rest assured that its reporting provisions as
required by Government Code Section 87302-1/ are accurate. We did
see a few items which need to be added to the appendix of
designated employees and wish to bring these to your attention.
You advised us that your firm has been retained to represent the
district in this code amendment process. This letter is therefore
being addressed to you.

We compared the district's code to the code of the East Bay
Regional Park District, another agency structured similarly to
your district. That park district designates several positions
within its code and has gone through a lengthy thought process of
individualizing disclosure to the decisions made by the respective
designated employees. We have enclosed a copy of the code so you
may review its content to determine if your code needs to be
expanded to include more positions.

In addition, your code does not provide for the disclosure of
financial interests by consultants for the district. The term
"designated employee" means any officer, employee, member, or
consultant of any agency whose position entails the making or
participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Page Two
April 30, 1990

have a material effect of any financial interest. (Section
82019.) A consultant is any natural person who provides under
contract, information, advice, recommendation or counsel to any
agency. (Regulation 18700.)

The Commission realizes that not all consultants participate
in the making of decisions on behalf of public agencies. Rather
than amend your code each time you retain a consultant that is in
a decision-making capacity, you may use a specialized disclosure
category that provides that the disclosure required of consultants
shall be determined on a case-by-case basis by the general manager
for the district. The general manager makes a written
determination as to what disclosure, if any, is required by any
particular consultant. A copy of this consultant disclosure
category language is attached, and should be incorporated into
your code.

The position "Consultant*" would be the last position
designated in the appendix. The asterisk would bring the reader
down to the consultant disclosure category at the bottom of that
page.

It was brought to our attention that your code does not
include the general counsel of the district. The Commission has
continually advised that when an attorney provides general
advice and counsel to an agency on a continuing basis (as may be the case
here), that attorney is a consultant within the meaning of the
Act. As such, the attorneys within a firm providing advice and
counsel to the district must file statements of economic
interests. (Frederick Advice Letter I-86-047, Alburquerque Advice
Letter A-85-244, and Gifford Advice Letter A-85-201, copies
enclosed.)

The district's general counsel is the type of consultant who
would be covered by the consultant disclosure category and file
statements of economic interests.

I hope this information is helpful to you. If you need any
additional assistance in amending your code or wish to discuss
this, please feel free to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

KED/JET/jt
Enclosures

cc. Anne McGowan, Attorney at Law
Gary Tate, Monterey Peninsula Regional Park District
